title: Procedural Archery Target Practice
prompt:
Create a single HTML file with an interactive 3D archery target practice game using Three.js and GLTFLoader.

## Scene Setup
- Use a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 2, 8) looking toward (0, 2, -20).
- Add a directional light at (5, 10, 5) with intensity 1.0 and an ambient light with intensity 0.4.
- Create a ground plane (100x100, green #3a7d3a) at y=0.
- Create a procedural archery target at position (0, 2.5, -15): 5 concentric rings using CylinderGeometry (very thin, like discs) with radii [2.0, 1.6, 1.2, 0.8, 0.4] and colors [white, black, blue, red, gold/yellow]. The target faces the camera (rotate so flat face points toward +Z). Each ring scores [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] points (outermost=1, bullseye=5).
- Load the 'BoxAnimated.glb' model using GLTFLoader. Place it at position (3, 0, -14) scaled to (0.8, 0.8, 0.8). Play its animation using AnimationMixer. This serves as a bonus animated target worth 10 points if hit.
- Create an arrow as a thin CylinderGeometry (radius 0.03, height 1.5) colored brown (#8B4513) with a small cone tip (dark gray). The arrow starts invisible.

## Crosshair & Aiming
- Display a crosshair in the center of the screen using a DOM overlay (two thin lines forming a +, white with slight opacity).
- Mouse movement controls aim direction. Track normalized mouse position (-1 to 1 range). Use pointer lock or simply track mousemove to offset the camera's lookAt point slightly. Store aimX and aimY in state.

## Shooting Mechanics
- Press SPACE to shoot an arrow. The arrow spawns at camera position and travels in the aim direction at speed 30 units/sec.
- Implement a simple power/charge: holding SPACE charges power from 0 to 1 over 2 seconds. Releasing SPACE fires. Power affects arrow speed: finalSpeed = 15 + 25 * power.
- Arrow follows a parabolic trajectory (gravity = -9.8 on Y axis applied to arrow velocity).
- When arrow collides with the target plane (z <= -14.5 approximately), check distance from target center to determine ring hit and score.
- When arrow collides with the BoxAnimated model's bounding box, award 10 bonus points.
- Arrows that miss (hit ground or go past z=-20) score 0.
- Maximum 10 arrows per round.

## Wind System
- Generate a random wind vector each round: windX in range [-2, 2], windZ in range [-1, 1]. Display wind as 'Wind: X.X, Z.Z' on the HUD.
- Wind applies a constant lateral force to the arrow during flight.

## HUD / UI
- Top-left: 'Score: 0' (id='hud-score')
- Top-right: 'Arrows: 10' (id='hud-arrows')
- Center-bottom: Power bar (id='power-bar') - a div that fills from 0% to 100% width, colored from green to red.
- Below score: 'Wind: X.X, Z.Z' (id='hud-wind')
- A 'New Round' button (id='btn-new-round') appears when all arrows are spent.
- A 'Last Hit' display (id='hud-last-hit') showing the ring/score of the last arrow.

## State Management
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ with:
- score: total score (number)
- arrowsLeft: remaining arrows (number, starts 10)
- arrowsFired: number of arrows fired
- power: current charge power 0-1 (number)
- isCharging: boolean, true while SPACE held
- isFlying: boolean, true while arrow is in flight
- lastHitScore: score of last arrow (0-5 or 10)
- lastHitDistance: distance from target center of last hit
- windX: current wind X component
- windZ: current wind Z component
- roundOver: boolean, true when arrowsLeft === 0 and no arrow flying
- targetHits: number of arrows that hit the circular target (score >= 1)
- bonusHits: number of arrows that hit the animated box
- arrowPosX: current arrow X position (or null)
- arrowPosY: current arrow Y position (or null)
- arrowPosZ: current arrow Z position (or null)
- boxAnimationPlaying: boolean, true if BoxAnimated animation is running

Update __3D_STATE__ every frame in the animation loop.
physics_constraints: Arrows follow parabolic trajectory with gravity=-9.8 m/s^2 on Y axis. Wind applies constant lateral acceleration to arrows during flight. Arrow speed determined by charge power: finalSpeed = 15 + 25*power. Collision detection against target plane at z~=-14.5 and box bounding volume.
